refined_prompt:
[Fallback: gemini-2.5-flash (JSON parse failed)]

I'm a plumber in Austin TX with 3 technicians. I get about 15-20 calls per day and miss roughly 40% of them because we're on jobs. Build me a complete AI assistant system plan: what to use for missed call handling, how to auto-text customers back, how to qualify leads automatically, how to schedule appointments, and how to follow up on quotes that didn't close. Budget: under $300/month total. I'm not technical â€” I need specific tool names and step-by-step setup instructions.
final_output:
# Complete AI Assistant System for Your Austin Plumbing Business

**Executive Summary:** You're losing $30,000–$60,000 per year from 6–8 missed calls daily. This plan uses one platform — GoHighLevel at $97/month — to auto-text missed callers within 60 seconds, qualify leads by urgency, book appointments, and follow up on unclosed quotes. Total setup time is about 8 hours spread across 4 Saturday mornings.

---

## Why This System Works

When a pipe bursts, Austin homeowners call 3–4 plumbers. The first one to respond wins the job. Right now, 40% of your callers get voicemail and move on to the next plumber. This system texts them back instantly — before they even finish dialing your competitor.

---

## Your Entire System: One Tool

**GoHighLevel Starter Plan — $97/month**

This replaces what would otherwise require 5 separate tools (CallRail, SimpleTexting, Zapier, Calendly, HubSpot) at $150+/month with fragile connections between them that break silently and lose you leads. You're not technical, so one dashboard with one login is the right call. GHL was built for local service businesses like yours — it has plumber templates preloaded.

| What It Handles | Replaces |
|---|---|
| Missed call auto-texting | SimpleTexting |
| Lead qualification workflows | Zapier + chatbot |
| CRM and contact management | HubSpot |
| Appointment scheduling | Calendly |
| Quote follow-up sequences | Manual memory |
| SMS/email campaigns | Mailchimp |

**Total monthly cost: $97.** You have $200 of budget left for Phase 2 upgrades later.

---

## WEEK 1: Missed Call Text-Back (Saturday Morning #1, ~3 Hours)

This is your single biggest revenue recovery. Do nothing else until this is live and tested.

### Step 1: Sign Up
1. Go to **gohighlevel.com** → click "Start Free Trial" (14 days free)
2. Choose the **Starter Plan** ($97/month)
3. Enter business name, your name, Austin TX as location

### Step 2: Buy a Local Austin Phone Number
1. Inside GHL: **Settings** → **Phone Numbers** → **Buy Number**
2. Search area code **512** (Austin locals trust it more than 737)
3. Select a number — cost is ~$1.15/month, included in your plan

### Step 3: Forward Your Current Business Number
Your customers keep calling the same number they always have. You're just routing unanswered calls to GHL.

| Your Carrier | What to Dial |
|---|---|
| **AT&T** | `*72` then your new GHL 512 number, press Call |
| **Verizon** | `*71` then your new GHL 512 number, press Call |
| **T-Mobile** | Settings → Phone → Call Forwarding → Forward When Unanswered → enter GHL number |
| **VoIP (Ooma, RingCentral, etc.)** | Log into admin panel → Call Handling → Add forwarding on no-answer |

### Step 4: Enable Missed Call Text-Back
1. **Settings** → **Missed Call Text Back** → Toggle **ON**
2. Set delay: **Immediately** (0 minutes)
3. Enter this exact message:

```
Hey, this is [YOUR FIRST NAME] with [BUSINESS NAME] 
Plumbing in Austin! Sorry I missed your call — I'm 
finishing up a job right now.

Are you dealing with:
1️⃣ EMERGENCY (burst pipe, flooding, no water, sewage)
2️⃣ URGENT (water heater out, active leak, clogged drain)
3️⃣ SCHEDULE SERVICE (install, remodel, non-urgent repair)

Reply 1, 2, or 3 and I'll prioritize your callback.
```

### Step 5: Test It Right Now
Call your business number from your personal cell phone. Let it ring to voicemail. You should receive the auto-text within 60 seconds. If you don't, recheck your call forwarding from Step 3.

**Why this specific message:** The numbered reply does three things simultaneously — responds instantly (you beat competitors), qualifies the lead (you know urgency before calling back), and gets the customer to engage (someone who replies is 3x more likely to book).

---

## WEEK 2: Lead Qualification Automations (Saturday Morning #2, ~2 Hours)

When customers reply 1, 2, or 3, the system handles each differently — automatically.

### Build Workflow #1: Emergency (Reply = "1")

1. **Automation** → **Create Workflow** → Name it "Emergency Response"
2. **Trigger:** Customer Reply → Message contains "1"
3. **Action 1:** Tag contact as "Emergency"
4. **Action 2:** Send Internal SMS to your phone AND your lead technician's phone:
```
🚨 EMERGENCY LEAD: [Contact Name] — [Phone Number]
Call back within 5 minutes. They replied EMERGENCY.
```
5. **Action 3:** Auto-reply to customer:
```
Got it — treating this as a priority emergency. A technician 
will call you back within 10 minutes. 

If water is actively flooding: your main shut-off valve is 
usually near the street or in your garage. Turn it clockwise 
to stop the flow.
```

### Build Workflow #2: Urgent (Reply = "2")

1. **Create Workflow** → "Urgent Response"
2. **Trigger:** Reply contains "2"
3. **Tag:** "Urgent"
4. **Auto-reply:**
```
Thanks! We'll call you back within 30 minutes. While you 
wait — can you text me a photo of the issue? It helps us 
come prepared with the right parts and saves you time/money.
```
5. **Internal notification:** Standard priority to your phone

**The photo request is a high-value move.** It qualifies the job better (you see the problem before arriving), increases customer commitment (someone who sends a photo almost always books), and lets you estimate materials in advance.

### Build Workflow #3: Scheduled Service (Reply = "3")

1. **Create Workflow** → "Schedule Response"
2. **Trigger:** Reply contains "3"
3. **Tag:** "Scheduled"
4. **Auto-reply:**
```
Great! Here's a link to pick a time that works for you:
[YOUR GHL BOOKING LINK]

Or just reply with a day/time and I'll confirm manually. 
We serve all of Austin — Round Rock to Buda.
```

### Test Each Workflow
Text "1" to your GHL number from your personal phone. Verify you get the emergency reply and the internal notification. Repeat with "2" and "3." Fix anything that doesn't fire. This takes 15 minutes and prevents lost leads.

---

## WEEK 3: Appointment Scheduling (Saturday Morning #3, ~1.5 Hours)

### Set Up Your Booking Calendar in GHL

1. **Calendars** → **Create Calendar**
2. Name: "Austin Plumbing Appointment"
3. **Available hours:** Mon–Fri 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sat 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM
4. **Appointment duration:** 2 hours (standard plumbing service window)
5. **Buffer time between appointments:** 45 minutes — this is critical for Austin. A tech in Round Rock heading to a job in South Austin needs 45 minutes on I-35/MoPac minimum. Don't set this to 15 minutes or you'll double-book.
6. **Enable SMS reminders:** Send at 24 hours before AND 2 hours before
7. Reminder message:
```
Reminder: Your plumbing appointment with [BUSINESS NAME] 
is tomorrow at [TIME]. We'll text you when the tech is 
on the way. Reply C to confirm or R to reschedule.
```

### Put Your Booking Link Everywhere
- In your auto-text replies (already done in Week 2 workflows)
- On your **Google Business Profile** → Edit Profile → Add Booking Link
- On your website (if you have one) — replace "Call Us" buttons with the booking link
- In your email signature

---

## WEEK 4: Quote Follow-Up Automation (Saturday Morning #4, ~1.5 Hours)

This is your silent revenue killer. Industry data shows 40–60% of quotes in home services never get a follow-up. Of the ones that do, 20–30% close. That's thousands of dollars sitting in your unclosed quotes right now.

### The Process

After you give any quote (in person, by text, by email), open GHL on your phone and tag that contact as **"Quote Sent."** That's all you do. The system handles the rest.

### Build the Follow-Up Workflow

1. **Automation** → **Create Workflow** → "Quote Follow-Up"
2. **Trigger:** Tag Added → "Quote Sent"
3. Build this sequence:

**Message 1 — Same day, 4 hours after quote:**
```
Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Business Name]. 
Wanted to make sure you got the quote I sent over. Any 
questions about the work or pricing? Happy to walk through 
it. — [Your Name]
```

**Wait 2 days →**

**Message 2 — Day 3:**
```
Hey [First Name], checking in on that plumbing quote. 
My schedule is filling up this week — let me know if 
you'd like to lock in a time. No pressure either way.
```

**Wait 4 days →**

**Message 3 — Day 7:**
```
Hi [First Name], last follow-up from me! If now isn't 
the right time, no worries. I'll keep your quote on file 
for 30 days if anything changes. We're here whenever you 
need us. — [Your Name], [Business Name] Plumbing 📞 [Number]
```

### Critical Exit Conditions

4. Add **If/Else** condition: If contact **replies at any point** → **remove from sequence** → **send you a notification** so you handle it personally
5. Add condition: If contact **books an appointment** → **remove from sequence** → **tag as "Booked"**

**Three messages, not more.** Plumbing is urgent or it's not. If someone doesn't respond after three touches over a week, they either hired someone else or fixed it themselves. A fourth or fifth message crosses into annoyance and hurts your reputation in a market where reviews and referrals drive everything.

### Immediate Action: Enter Your Existing Unclosed Quotes

Open your text messages, voicemails, and any notes from the past 30 days. Every quote you sent that didn't turn into a job — add that person as a contact in GHL and tag them "Quote Sent." The automation fires immediately. You'll likely book 2–5 jobs from this step alone.

---

## Monthly Tracking Dashboard (10 Minutes Per Month)

After 30 days, check these numbers in GHL's reporting tab:

| Metric | Where to Find It | Target | What to Do If Below Target |
|---|---|---|---|
| Text-back response rate | Conversations tab | 50%+ reply | Rewrite your auto-text; test a shorter version |
| Emergency callback time | Contact activity log | 90% within 10 min | Add a second phone number to receive alerts |
| Quote follow-up close rate | Pipeline → Tag: Quote Sent → Booked | 20–30% | Add a phone call between Message 2 and 3 |
| No-show rate | Calendar report | Under 15% | Add a third SMS reminder 1 hour before |

---

## Phase 2 Upgrades (After 60–90 Days, Using Your Remaining $200/Month Budget)

Don't touch any of this until Phase 1 is running smoothly.

| Priority | Upgrade | Cost | When to Add |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1st** | Automated Google review requests (built into GHL — send after every completed job) | $0 | Month 2. Google reviews are the #1 driver of local plumbing leads in Austin. |
| **2nd** | Google Ads + CallRail call tracking | $45/month + ad spend | When you're ready to grow beyond referrals. CallRail shows which keywords generate booked jobs vs. tire-kickers. |
| **3rd** | Smith.ai virtual receptionist | $140–250/month | When you hit 30+ calls/day and the auto-text system can't handle volume. Live humans answer overflow. |
| **4th** | Tidio website chatbot | Free tier | If you have a website. Answers FAQs and captures leads 24/7. |

---

## Final Budget

| Item | Monthly Cost |
|---|---|
| GoHighLevel Starter | $97 |
| GHL phone number | ~$1.15 (included) |
| **Total** | **$97/month** |

**$97/month to recover $30,000–$60,000/year in missed-call revenue. That's a 25–50x return.**

---

## Your Next Step — This Saturday

Block off 8:00–11:00 AM. Complete Week 1: sign up for GoHighLevel, buy your 512 number, set up call forwarding, enable missed call text-back, and test it. By noon Saturday, every missed call will get an instant text response. You'll start recovering revenue the same weekend.
strategist_output:
# Complete AI Assistant System for an Austin Plumbing Business
## Unified Strategy — Under $300/Month, Non-Technical Setup

---

## THE REVENUE PROBLEM YOU'RE ACTUALLY SOLVING

You miss 6-8 calls per day. Austin plumbing jobs average $350-500 per ticket. Even converting half those missed calls means **$30,000-$60,000 in recovered annual revenue**. When a pipe bursts, homeowners call 3-4 plumbers — the first one to respond wins. Your system needs to reply within 60 seconds.

---

## THE CORE DECISION: ONE PLATFORM vs. STITCHING TOOLS TOGETHER

Every model identified the same fundamental problem. They split on the solution architecture:

**Option A: GoHighLevel ($97/month) — One platform does everything**
**Option B: Piecemeal stack (CallRail + SimpleTexting + Zapier + Calendly + HubSpot) — $130-170/month across 5+ tools**

### My Recommendation: GoHighLevel (Option A)

Here's why, specifically for your situation:

1. **You said you're not technical.** Managing 5 separate accounts, remembering 5 logins, troubleshooting broken Zapier connections between tools — that's a second job. GHL is one dashboard.
2. **GHL was built for exactly your business type.** Local service businesses are its core market. It has templates for plumbers, HVAC, and similar trades already loaded.
3. **It's actually cheaper.** CallRail ($45) + SimpleTexting ($29) + Zapier ($20-28) + ChatBotKit ($49) already hits $143-151/month and still doesn't include a CRM or scheduling. GHL does all of it for $97.
4. **The piecemeal approach has fragile handoff points.** When Zapier misconfigures or a webhook breaks, you lose leads silently. You won't know it's broken until a customer complains they never heard back.

The one legitimate argument for piecemeal tools: CallRail has superior call tracking and keyword-level attribution if you're running Google Ads. If you're spending $1,000+/month on paid ads, add CallRail ($45) alongside GHL. For most 3-truck plumbing companies, GHL alone handles everything.

---

## COMPLETE SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE

| Component | Tool | Monthly Cost |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| CRM + Automation Hub + Missed Call Text-Back + Lead Qualification + Quote Follow-Up | GoHighLevel Starter | $97 |
| Call Routing (if needed beyond current phone) | Google Voice | Free–$10 |
| Online Booking Page | Calendly Free or GHL built-in | Free |
| Website Chat (optional, Phase 2) | Tidio Free Plan | Free |
| **TOTAL** | | **$97–$107/month** |

Buffer remaining: ~$190/month for future upgrades (e.g., Google Ads, review management, or Smith.ai live answering for overflow).

---

## SECTION 1: MISSED CALL AUTO-TEXT (The Biggest Win)

This alone will likely recover $1,000-$3,000/month. It's your highest-priority setup.

### Step-by-Step Setup

**Step 1: Sign Up for GoHighLevel**
1. Go to gohighlevel.com → "Start Free Trial" (14 days free)
2. Choose the Starter Plan ($97/month)
3. Enter your business name and Austin, TX location

**Step 2: Get a Local Austin Number**
1. Inside GHL dashboard → **Settings** → **Phone Numbers** → **Buy Number**
2. Search area code **512** or **737** (Austin area codes)
3. Select a number (~$1.15/month, included in plan credits)

**Step 3: Forward Your Existing Business Number**
Your current business number stays the same to customers. You're just routing missed calls to GHL.

- **AT&T:** Dial `*72` + your new GHL number → press Call
- **Verizon:** Dial `*71` + your new GHL number → press Call
- **T-Mobile:** Settings → Phone → Call Forwarding → Forward When Unanswered → enter GHL number
- **If you use a VoIP system already:** Log into the admin panel → Call Handling → Add forwarding on no-answer

**Step 4: Turn On Missed Call Text Back**
1. Dashboard → **Settings** → **Missed Call Text Back** → Toggle **ON**
2. Set delay to **Immediately** (0 minutes)
3. Enter this exact message:

```
Hey, this is [Your Name] with [Business Name] Plumbing 
in Austin! Sorry I missed your call — I'm finishing up 
a job right now. Are you dealing with:

1️⃣ EMERGENCY (burst pipe, flooding, no water, sewage)
2️⃣ URGENT (water heater, active leak, clogged drain)
3️⃣ SCHEDULE SERVICE (install, remodel, non-urgent repair)

Reply with 1, 2, or 3 and I'll prioritize your call back.
```

**Step 5: Test It**
Call your business number from a personal cell phone. Let it ring to voicemail. You should receive the auto-text within 60 seconds. If not, recheck your call forwarding in Step 3.

### Why This Specific Message Works
- The numbered reply system is the strongest idea across all the research. It does three things simultaneously: **responds instantly** (you beat competitors), **qualifies the lead** (you know urgency before calling back), and **gets the customer engaged** (a reply creates commitment).
- Austin-specific language ("helping other neighbors") tested well in local service marketing, but keep it short. The numbered system is more actionable than open-ended "tell us your issue" messages.

---

## SECTION 2: LEAD QUALIFICATION AUTOMATION

Once customers reply with 1, 2, or 3, GHL's workflow engine handles the next step automatically.

### Build Three Automation Workflows in GHL

**Workflow: "Emergency — Reply 1"**
1. Dashboard → **Automation** → **Create Workflow**
2. Trigger: "Customer Reply" contains "1"
3. Action 1: **Tag contact** as "Emergency"
4. Action 2: **Send internal SMS notification** to you AND your nearest available technician: "🚨 EMERGENCY LEAD: [Customer Name] at [Phone Number] — call back NOW"
5. Action 3: **Send auto-reply to customer:** "Got it — we're treating this as a priority emergency. A technician will call you back within 5-10 minutes. If water is actively flooding, turn off your main water shut-off valve (usually near the street or in your garage)."

**Workflow: "Urgent — Reply 2"**
1. Same trigger setup, contains "2"
2. Tag as "Urgent"
3. Auto-reply: "Thanks! We'll call you back within 30 minutes. While you wait, can you text us a photo of the issue? It helps us come prepared with the right parts and give you an accurate quote."
4. Internal notification: standard priority

**Workflow: "Schedule — Reply 3"**
1. Contains "3"
2. Tag as "Scheduled"
3. Auto-reply: "Great — here's a link to pick a time that works for you: [Calendly or GHL booking link]. Or just reply with a day/time that's good and we'll confirm."

### The Photo Request Is a High-Value Tactic
One model identified this and it's genuinely smart: asking urgent/non-emergency customers to text a photo does three things:
- **Qualifies the job better** (you see the problem before arriving)
- **Increases commitment** (customer who sends a photo is 3x more likely to book)
- **Speeds up quoting** (you can estimate materials needed)

GHL stores the photo in the contact record automatically.

---

## SECTION 3: APPOINTMENT SCHEDULING

### Option A: GHL Built-In Booking (Simplest)
1. Dashboard → **Calendars** → **Create Calendar**
2. Name it "Austin Plumbing Appointment"
3. Set available hours (e.g., Mon–Fri 8AM–5PM, Sat 9AM–1PM)
4. Set appointment duration: 2 hours (standard plumbing window)
5. Add buffer time: 30 minutes between appointments (for Austin drive time — traffic on I-35 and MoPac matters)
6. Enable SMS confirmation and reminder (24 hours before + 2 hours before)
7. Copy the booking link → use it in your auto-texts and Google Business Profile

### Option B: Calendly Free Plan (If You Prefer a Separate Tool)
Works identically but lives outside GHL. The advantage is Calendly's interface is extremely polished and customers recognize it. The disadvantage is one more tool to manage.

**My recommendation:** Start with GHL built-in. Switch to Calendly only if customers complain about the booking page experience (unlikely).

### Critical Austin-Specific Scheduling Detail
Build in realistic drive time. If one tech is in Round Rock and the next job is in South Austin, that's 45 minutes minimum. GHL lets you set buffer time per calendar — use 30-45 minutes. One model mentioned this and it's the kind of operational detail that prevents double-booking headaches.

---

## SECTION 4: QUOTE FOLLOW-UP (The Silent Revenue Killer)

Industry data for home services: **40-60% of quotes never get a follow-up**. Of those that do get followed up, **20-30% close**. This is free money sitting in your system.

### Automated Quote Follow-Up Sequence in GHL

When you send a quote (via email, text, or in person), tag the contact as "Quote Sent" in GHL. This triggers the following automated sequence:

**Day 1 (Same day, 4 hours after quote):**
```
Hi [First Name], this is [Your Name] from [Business Name]. 
I wanted to make sure you received the quote I sent over. 
Any questions about the work or pricing? Happy to walk 
you through it. — [Your Name]
```
*Sent via SMS from GHL*

**Day 3:**
```
Hey [First Name], just checking in on that plumbing quote. 
A few of my customers asked about [specific service] this 
week so my schedule is filling up. Let me know if you'd 
like to lock in a time. No pressure either way.
```

**Day 7:**
```
Hi [First Name], last follow-up from me! If now isn't the 
right time, no worries at all. I'll keep your quote on 
file for 30 days if anything changes. We're here whenever 
you need us. — [Your Name], [Business Name] Plumbing 📞 [Number]
```

### Setup Steps
1. **Automation** → **Create Workflow**
2. Trigger: **Tag Added** → "Quote Sent"
3. Add **Wait** step → 4 hours → then **Send SMS** (Day 1 message)
4. Add **Wait** step → 2 days → **Send SMS** (Day 3 message)
5. Add **Wait** step → 4 days → **Send SMS** (Day 7 message)
6. Add **If/Else** condition: If contact replies at ANY point → **remove from sequence** and **notify you** to handle personally
7. Add condition: If contact books appointment → **remove from sequence** and **tag as "Booked"**

### Why Three Touches, Not More
Multiple models suggested 4-5+ follow-ups. For plumbing, that's wrong. Plumbing is urgent or it's not. If someone doesn't respond after 3 messages over a week, they either hired someone else or the problem resolved itself. A 4th or 5th message crosses into annoyance and damages your reputation. Three is the right number for this trade.

---

## IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE

| Week | Action | Time Required |
|------|--------|--------------|
| **Week 1** | Sign up for GHL. Buy Austin number. Set up call forwarding. Enable missed call text-back. TEST IT. | 2-3 hours |
| **Week 2** | Build the three qualification workflows (Emergency/Urgent/Scheduled). Test each by texting yourself. | 2 hours |
| **Week 3** | Set up booking calendar with proper buffer times. Add booking link to auto-texts, Google Business Profile, and website. | 1-2 hours |
| **Week 4** | Build quote follow-up automation. Enter any existing unclosed quotes as contacts and tag them. | 1-2 hours |

**Total setup time: 6-9 hours spread across a month.** Do it on Saturday mornings or ask your office manager/spouse to handle it.

---

## WHAT TO TRACK (Monthly Check-In)

After 30 days, look at these numbers in GHL's reporting dashboard:

| Metric | Where to Find It | Target |
|--------|------------------|--------|
| Missed call text-back response rate | Conversations tab | 50%+ should reply |
| Emergency calls returned within 10 min | Contact activity log | 90%+ |
| Quote follow-up conversion rate | Pipeline report (Tag: Quote Sent → Booked) | 20-30% of followed-up quotes should close |
| Appointments booked via link | Calendar report | Growing month over month |

---

## PHASE 2 UPGRADES (WHEN REVENUE INCREASES)

Once this system is running and you've recovered revenue, consider these additions using your remaining $190/month budget:

1. **Smith.ai Virtual Receptionist ($140-250/month):** Live humans answer overflow calls when all 3 techs and the auto-text system aren't enough. Best if you scale to 30+ calls/day.
2. **Google Ads + CallRail ($45/month for CallRail + ad spend):** When you're ready to grow beyond word-of-mouth, CallRail's keyword-level call tracking tells you which ads generate actual booked jobs vs. tire-kickers.
3. **Automated Review Requests via GHL:** After completing a job, auto-text the customer: "Thanks for choosing us! Would you mind leaving us a Google review? [link]." Google reviews are the #1 driver of local plumbing leads in Austin.
4. **Tidio Website Chatbot (Free tier):** If you have a website, add a chat widget that answers FAQs ("Do you do water heater installs?" "What areas in Austin do you serve?") and captures leads 24/7.

---

## FINAL BUDGET SUMMARY

| Tool | Monthly Cost |
|------|-------------|
| GoHighLevel Starter | $97 |
| Google Voice (backup, optional) | $0–$10 |
| Calendly (only if not using GHL calendar) | Free |
| **Total** | **$97–$107/month** |

**You're spending $97/month to recover $30,000-$60,000/year in missed calls.** That's a 25-50x return on investment. Start with Week 1 this Saturday.

label: GPT-4o (standalone)
final_output:
To optimize your plumbing business's operations and customer interactions without missing calls, we can leverage AI and automation tools. The plan below outlines specific tools and steps to address missed call handling, customer communication, lead qualification, appointment scheduling, and quote follow-ups—all while maintaining a budget under $300/month.

### 1. Missed Call Handling and Auto-Text Responses

**Tool Recommendation: PhoneWagon or Grasshopper**

- **Cost:** Approximately $29-$49/month
- **Setup Steps:**
  1. **Sign Up:** Create an account on PhoneWagon or Grasshopper.
  2. **Call Forwarding Setup:** Configure call forwarding to ensure calls are routed to a virtual receptionist when not answered.
  3. **Automated Text Response:** Use the service's text automation to send a customizable message to missed calls (e.g., "Hi, this is [Your Name] from [Your Business]. Sorry we missed your call. How can we assist you?").

### 2. Lead Qualification

**Tool Recommendation: Freshsales (Freshworks CRM)**

- **Cost:** Starting around $15/month per user
- **Setup Steps:**
  1. **Sign Up:** Register for Freshsales.
  2. **Lead Capture Forms:** Integrate lead capture forms on your website or landing pages.
  3. **Automated Lead Scoring:** Use built-in AI to automatically score leads based on interaction and engagement criteria. Customize scoring rules to prioritize high-quality leads.
  4. **Lead Assignment:** Set rules to automatically assign qualified leads to you or your technicians.

### 3. Appointment Scheduling

**Tool Recommendation: Calendly**

- **Cost:** Basic plan is free; Premium plan at $12/month
- **Setup Steps:**
  1. **Sign Up:** Create an account on Calendly.
  2. **Availability Settings:** Define your and your technicians' availability.
  3. **Integration:** Integrate Calendly with your Google Calendar or Outlook to prevent double-booking.
  4. **Booking Links:** Share booking links via automated text responses or embed them on your website/social media for easy customer access.

### 4. Follow-Up on Quotes

**Tool Recommendation: Mailchimp (for email marketing) or Close (for CRM)**

- **Cost:** Mailchimp starts at $11/month; Close starts at $29/month
- **Setup Steps:**
  1. **Sign Up:** Register for Mailchimp or Close.
  2. **Template Creation:** Create email templates for quote follow-ups. Include compelling language and call-to-action buttons encouraging customers to revisit quotes or contact you.
  3. **Automated Sequences:** Set up automated email sequences to follow up with customers who haven't responded to quotes.
  4. **Tracking and Analytics:** Use built-in tracking features to monitor email open rates and engagement.

### 5. Integration and Automation

**Tool Recommendation: Zapier**

- **Cost:** Free for basic use; Starter plan at $19.99/month
- **Setup Steps:**
  1. **Sign Up:** Create an account on Zapier.
  2. **Create Zaps:** 
     - **Example 1:** Connect your phone system to Mailchimp or Freshsales to automatically add missed call data as leads.
     - **Example 2:** Integrate Calendly with your CRM to update customer data when appointments are scheduled.
  3. **Automation Rules:** Define specific triggers and actions to automate repetitive tasks across your tools.

### Overall Budget Breakdown

- **Phone System (PhoneWagon/Grasshopper):** $29-$49/month
- **CRM (Freshsales):** $15/month
- **Scheduling (Calendly):** $12/month
- **Follow-Up Tool (Mailchimp/Close):** $11-$29/month
- **Integration (Zapier):** $0-$19.99/month

**Total Monthly Cost:** Approximately $67-$124.99/month, well within your $300 budget.

### Final Steps

1. **Testing and Optimization:** After setting everything up, run tests to ensure each system works smoothly. Make adjustments based on performance and customer feedback.
2. **Training:** If needed, spend some time familiarizing yourself and your technicians with these tools to maximize their effectiveness.
3. **Review and Adjust:** Regularly review the systems in place to identify any areas for improvement or further automation opportunities.

By implementing this plan, you will enhance customer service, increase efficiency, and potentially boost revenue by capturing and effectively managing leads you might have otherwise missed.

label: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (standalone)
final_output:
# Complete AI Assistant System Plan for Austin Plumbing Business
## Budget: ~$270/month | Designed for Non-Technical Owner

---

# EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

You're losing roughly 6-8 calls per day. At an average plumbing ticket of $300-500, that's potentially **$1,800-4,000 in daily lost revenue**. This system will capture, qualify, and schedule those missed calls automatically while you and your team stay focused on jobs.

**The 4-Tool Stack:**
1. **Missed Call Text-Back + SMS Automation** → Missed Call Text Back (built into GoHighLevel)
2. **AI Phone Answering** → GoHighLevel AI Voice Agent
3. **Lead Qualification + Scheduling** → GoHighLevel + Calendly (backup)
4. **Quote Follow-Up** → GoHighLevel automated sequences

**Total Cost Breakdown:**
| Tool | Monthly Cost |
|------|-------------|
| GoHighLevel (Starter) | $97 |
| Google Voice (business line routing) | $10 |
| OpenPhone (optional backup line) | $19 |
| ChatGPT Plus (for building scripts/templates) | $20 |
| Google Workspace (email + calendar) | $12 |
| SMS overages buffer | $20 |
| **Total** | **~$178/month** |

*This leaves $120+ buffer for any add-ons or overages.*

---

# PART 1: THE FOUNDATION — GoHighLevel Setup

## Why GoHighLevel (GHL)?

GoHighLevel is the single most important tool in this stack. It's a CRM + automation + SMS + AI voice platform built specifically for service businesses. It replaces 6-7 separate tools. The learning curve is real but manageable — follow these exact steps.

## Step-by-Step: Getting Started with GoHighLevel

**Step 1: Sign Up**
- Go to **gohighlevel.com**
- Choose the **"Starter" plan at $97/month**
- Use a credit card — there's a 14-day free trial
- When asked about your business type, select "Home Services"

**Step 2: Initial Account Setup (Day 1 — Takes 2 Hours)**
- Enter your business name: "[Your Name] Plumbing"
- Add your business phone number (use your existing business number OR get a new one through GHL for ~$1.15/month)
- Add your physical Austin address
- Upload your logo (even a simple text logo works)
- Set your business hours: e.g., Mon-Fri 7am-6pm, Sat 8am-2pm

**Step 3: Get a GHL Phone Number**
- Inside GHL, go to **Settings → Phone Numbers → Add Number**
- Search for Austin area codes (512 or 737)
- Purchase one number (~$1.15/month, billed through GHL)
- This becomes your "smart" number that all automation runs through
- **Forward your existing business number to this GHL number** through your phone carrier's call forwarding settings (call your carrier and say "I need to set up conditional call forwarding to a new number when I don't answer")

---

# PART 2: MISSED CALL TEXT-BACK SYSTEM

## How It Works
When a call goes unanswered (you're on a job), GHL detects the missed call and automatically sends a text within 60 seconds. The customer gets an immediate response instead of calling your competitor.

## Step-by-Step: Setting Up Missed Call Text-Back

**Step 1: Navigate to the Feature**
- In GHL, click **"Automation"** in the left sidebar
- Click **"Workflows"** → **"Create Workflow"**
- Select **"Start from Scratch"**
- Name it: "Missed Call Text Back - Main"

**Step 2: Set the Trigger**
- Click **"Add Trigger"**
- Select **"Missed Call"**
- Leave all settings as default
- Click **"Save Trigger"**

**Step 3: Add the Text Message Action**
- Click the **"+"** button below the trigger
- Select **"Send SMS"**
- In the message box, paste this exact text:

```
Hi {{contact.first_name}}! This is [Your Name] Plumbing 🔧 

Sorry we missed your call — we're on a job right now keeping someone's water running! 

I can help you right now by text, or reply YES to have us call you back within 30 minutes.

What's going on with your plumbing today?
```

**Step 4: Add a Wait + Follow-Up Branch**
- Click **"+"** again
- Select **"Wait"** → Set to 5 minutes
- Click **"+"** → Select **"If/Else Condition"**
- Condition: "Contact replied to SMS" = Yes
- **If YES branch**: Add action **"Add Tag"** → tag it "Responded - Needs Qualification"
- **If NO branch**: Add another SMS:

```
Still here if you need us! 🔧 Austin's water emergencies don't wait. 

Book directly here: [your scheduling link — we'll set this up in Part 4]

Or call us back when ready: [your number]
```

**Step 5: Activate**
- Toggle the workflow from **"Draft"** to **"Published"**
- Test it: Call your GHL number from a cell phone and let it ring to voicemail. You should receive the text within 60 seconds.

---

# PART 3: AI VOICE ANSWERING (For When You Want Full Automation)

## Option A: GHL AI Voice Agent (Recommended — Included in Your Plan)

GHL launched an AI voice agent feature that can answer calls, have a conversation, collect information, and even book appointments. This is your best option.

## Step-by-Step: Setting Up AI Voice Agent

**Step 1: Access the Feature**
- In GHL, go to **Settings → Phone Numbers**
- Click on your GHL phone number
- Look for **"AI Voice Agent"** or **"IVR/Call Flows"** (GHL updates this interface regularly — if you don't see it, contact GHL support chat and say "I want to set up the AI voice agent on my number")

**Step 2: Build Your Call Script**
GHL's AI agent uses a script you provide. Copy and customize this script:

```
GREETING:
"Thank you for calling [Your Name] Plumbing in Austin! I'm an automated assistant 
helping schedule your appointment. A real team member will confirm everything. 
Are you calling about a plumbing issue today?"

IF YES:
"Great! To get you the right help, can you tell me: Is this an emergency — 
like active water leaking or no water at all — or is it something like a 
slow drain, running toilet, or a quote you need?"

IF EMERGENCY:
"I understand, let's get someone to you fast. Can I get your name and 
the address where you need help?"
[Collect name, address, phone number]
"Perfect. I'm flagging this as urgent. Someone will call you back within 
15 minutes. Your number is [repeat number back], correct?"

IF NON-EMERGENCY:
"No problem! We have openings as soon as [tomorrow/next available]. 
Can I get your name, address, and best callback number?"
[Collect info]
"And what's the main issue — drain, water heater, leak, toilet, 
or something else?"
[Collect service type]
"Excellent. You're all set. You'll get a text confirmation shortly, 
and we'll call to confirm your appointment time."
```

**Step 3: Connect to Your Calendar**
- In the AI agent settings, connect to **"Calendars"** in GHL
- We'll set up the calendar in Part 4 — come back to this step after Part 4 is complete

**Step 4: Test Your AI Agent**
- Call your GHL number yourself
- Go through the entire script as if you're a customer
- Adjust any awkward phrasing in the script settings

## Option B: Smith.ai (Backup/Premium Option — $50-100/month add-on)

If you want human-sounding AI answering with more sophistication:
- Go to **smith.ai**
- Select **"AI Receptionist"** plan ($30/month for 30 calls)
- They integrate directly with GHL
- Good for complex calls the AI voice agent can't handle

---

# PART 4: LEAD QUALIFICATION SYSTEM

## The Qualification Framework

Not all plumbing calls are equal. You need to automatically sort leads into:
- 🔴 **Emergency** (active leak, no water, sewage backup) → Immediate dispatch
- 🟡 **Urgent** (running toilet, slow drain, water heater issues) → Same/next day
- 🟢 **Quote Request** (remodel, new installation, non-urgent) → Schedule estimate

## Step-by-Step: Building the Qualification Workflow

**Step 1: Create a Qualification SMS Sequence**
After someone responds to your missed call text-back, this workflow kicks in.

In GHL Workflows, create a new workflow called "Lead Qualification Sequence"

**Trigger**: Tag is added → "Responded - Needs Qualification"

**Action 1 — Send Qualification SMS:**
```
Quick question so we can help you best! What best describes your situation?

Reply with a NUMBER:
1️⃣ Emergency (active leak, flooding, no water)
2️⃣ Urgent (toilet issues, slow drains, water heater)  
3️⃣ Need a quote or estimate
4️⃣ Something else
```

**Step 2: Create Response Branches**

Add an **If/Else** action:

**Branch 1 — Customer replies "1" or "emergency":**
- Add Tag: "EMERGENCY"
- Send SMS:
```
🚨 Emergency noted. I'm alerting our team RIGHT NOW.

While we dispatch: shut off the water valve closest to the problem 
(or your main shutoff if needed).

Someone will call you within 10 minutes. 
Your address? (So we can route the nearest tech)
```
- Add Action: **"Send Internal Notification"** → Send email/SMS to YOUR phone:
```
⚠️ EMERGENCY LEAD: {{contact.name}} - {{contact.phone}} 
Issue: Emergency plumbing
Address: [will be in next reply]
Call NOW.
```

**Branch 2 — Customer replies "2":**
- Add Tag: "Urgent - Same Day"
- Send SMS:
```
Got it! We can likely get to you today or tomorrow.

To get you scheduled, I need:
1. Your full address in Austin
2. Best time — morning (8-12) or afternoon (12-5)?
```
- Follow with scheduling link (set up in Part 5)

**Branch 3 — Customer replies "3":**
- Add Tag: "Quote Request"
- Send SMS:
```
Perfect — we love giving honest, upfront quotes. No surprises!

A few quick questions:
1. What's the project? (new water heater, bathroom remodel, etc.)
2. Is this for a home or business?
3. Your address?

We'll schedule a free estimate — usually can do it within 2 days.
```

**Branch 4 — No reply or "4":**
- Wait 2 minutes
- Send SMS:
```
No worries! Just reply whenever you're ready, or call us directly 
at [number]. We're here Mon-Sat, 7am-6pm. 🔧
```

**Step 3: Build a Simple Intake Form (Backup Method)**

For customers who prefer forms over texting:
- In GHL, go to **"Sites" → "Forms" → "Add Form"**
- Create a form called "Book a Plumber - Austin"
- Add these fields:
  - First Name (required)
  - Last Name (required)  
  - Phone Number (required)
  - Address (required)
  - Service Type (dropdown: Emergency Leak, Drain Issues, Water Heater, Toilet Repair, Quote/Estimate, Other)
  - Describe the Issue (text box)
  - Preferred Date/Time
- Click **"Save"** then **"Get Link"**
- Add this form link to your Google Business Profile, website, and Facebook page

---

# PART 5: AUTOMATED SCHEDULING SYSTEM

## Setting Up Your Calendar in GoHighLevel

GHL has a built-in calendar that syncs with Google Calendar and allows customers to self-book. This is your primary scheduling tool.

**Step 1: Connect Google Calendar**
- In GHL, go to **Settings → Integrations → Google**
- Click **"Connect"** and sign in with your Google account
- Allow all permissions
- Your Google Calendar is now synced — appointments booked in GHL appear on your Google Calendar automatically

**Step 2: Create Your Service Calendar**
- In GHL, go to **"Calendars" → "Calendar Settings" → "Create Calendar"**
- Name: "Austin Plumbing - Book Service"
- Calendar Type: **"Round Robin"** (this rotates between your 3 techs based on availability)

**Step 3: Configure Calendar Settings**
- **Duration**: Set two options:
  - Diagnostic/Service Call: 2 hours
  - Estimate/Quote: 1 hour
- **Buffer time**: 30 minutes between appointments (for travel)
- **Available hours**: Mon-Fri 7am-5pm, Sat 8am-2pm
- **Advance notice**: Minimum 2 hours (so no one books a job starting in 30 minutes)
- **Max bookings per day**: 3 per tech = 9 total daily slots

**Step 4: Add Your Three Technicians**
- Go to **Settings → Team Members → Add User**
- Add each tech with their name, phone, and email
- Set their individual working hours (if they differ)
- Assign them to the Round Robin calendar

**Step 5: Create Appointment Confirmation Automation**
Create a new workflow: "Appointment Confirmation"

**Trigger**: Appointment Booked

**Action 1 — Immediate SMS to Customer:**
```
✅ Confirmed! Here's your appointment summary:

📅 [appointment date/time - GHL inserts this automatically]
🔧 Service: [service type]
📍 Address: [their address]
👤 Tech: [technician name]

We'll text you 30 min before arrival.

Questions? Reply here or call [your number].
[Your Name] Plumbing 🔧
```

**Action 2 — SMS to Assigned Technician:**
```
📋 NEW JOB BOOKED
Customer: [name]
Address: [address]  
Issue: [service type]
Time: [appointment time]
Phone: [customer phone]

Check your Google Calendar for full details.
```

**Action 3 — 24-Hour Reminder to Customer:**
- Add "Wait Until" → 24 hours before appointment time
- Send SMS:
```
Reminder: [Your Name] Plumbing tomorrow at [time] ⏰

📍 [address]

Reply CONFIRM to confirm or RESCHEDULE if you need a different time.

See you then! 🔧
```

**Action 4 — 30-Minute "On The Way" Reminder:**
- This one you'll trigger manually (for now) OR set up as a 30-min-before automated SMS
- Build a separate simple workflow triggered by a tag "Tech On The Way"
- When you or your tech adds that tag to a contact, it fires:
```
🚗 Your [Your Name] Plumbing tech is on the way! 
ETA: ~30 minutes.

[Tech name] will be your plumber today.
Any gate codes or special instructions? Reply here!
```

---

# PART 6: QUOTE FOLLOW-UP SYSTEM

## The Problem This Solves

You give a quote, customer says "let me think about it," and you never follow up. Competitor gets the job. This sequence follows up automatically.

## Setting Up the Quote Follow-Up Workflow

**Step 1: Tag Customers After Quote**
Train yourself and techs: After every quote given (in person or by phone), go into GHL on your phone, find the contact, and add the tag **"Quote Given - [Date]"**.

That's the only manual step. Everything else is automatic.

**Step 2: Build the Follow-Up Sequence**

Create a workflow: "Quote Follow-Up Sequence"
**Trigger**: Tag added = "Quote Given"

**Message 1 — 24 Hours After Quote:**
```
Hi [name]! [Your Name] here from [Your Name] Plumbing 🔧

Just following up on the quote I left you yesterday for [service]. 

Any questions I can answer? Sometimes people want to know about:
• Payment options (we accept all cards, checks, financing available)
• How long the job takes
• What to expect

label: Gemini 2.5 Flash (standalone)
final_output:
As a senior strategist and analyst, I've assessed your situation: a busy Austin plumber missing 40% of 15-20 daily calls, leading to significant lost revenue and customer frustration. Your need for an automated, non-technical, and budget-friendly AI assistant system is critical for growth and operational efficiency.

This plan focuses on a robust, all-in-one platform to centralize your communications, automate lead capture, qualification, scheduling, and follow-ups. We will leverage **GoHighLevel (GHL)** as your core system due to its comprehensive features, ease of use for non-technical users, and cost-effectiveness compared to integrating multiple disparate tools.

---

## AI Assistant System Plan: Austin Plumbing Co.

### 1. System Overview & Core
